editor_settings.title: Fujifilm X100VI vs Ricoh GR III: Which Compact Camera Makes More Sense?
editor_settings.content:
Fujifilm X100VI vs Ricoh GR III: Which Compact Camera Makes More Sense?

These cameras get grouped together all the time, but they are not trying to win the same way.

The X100VI is the more emotional buy. The GR III is the more ruthless one.

Quick Verdict

Buy the Fujifilm X100VI if you want the richer shooting experience, the 35mm equivalent look, the viewfinder-led design, stronger JPEG personality, and a camera that feels like an object you bond with.

Buy the Ricoh GR III if you want something smaller, simpler, and more focused on getting out of your way. It is a better stealth compact. The X100VI is a better indulgent compact.

The Core Difference

The X100VI gives you a fixed 23mm f/2 lens, which is a 35mm equivalent view, plus a 40.2MP APS-C sensor, up to 6 stops of IBIS, 20 film simulations, and 6.2K video.

The Ricoh GR III goes the opposite direction. Ricoh gives you a 24.24MP APS-C sensor, an 18.3mm f/2.8 lens with a 28mm equivalent view, and a much more brutally compact body with 3-axis sensor-shift stabilization.

One is about tactile pleasure. One is about disappearing into your pocket and making the shot.

Why the X100VI Wins for Some People

  • better straight-out-of-camera style if you love Fujifilm color
  • 35mm equivalent is easier for many people than 28mm
  • higher 40.2MP resolution gives more crop room
  • the body and controls make photography feel more intentional

If you care about experience, the X100VI is hard to beat.

Why the GR III Wins for Other People

  • it is much easier to carry truly everywhere
  • the 28mm equivalent view is brilliant for wide street and travel scenes
  • it feels less like a precious object and more like a tool

The GR III is the kind of camera that quietly becomes part of your life. The X100VI is the kind of camera that makes you want to talk about your camera.

The Price Difference Changes Everything

The X100VI would be easier to recommend universally if the market around it was normal. It is not.

As of May 3, 2026, the X100VI was still sitting around $1,799 new and showing backorder behavior. That alone makes the GR III feel like the more practical buy for a lot of people, even before you get into preferences.

If you still want the X100VI but want to dodge the worst of the pricing pain, this kind of used listing is the more sensible way to do it:

[product-card:fujifilm-x100vi-silver-camera-used-with-accessories-premium-compact-camera]


Which One Should You Actually Buy?

Get the X100VI if you want to enjoy the process as much as the output.

Get the GR III if you want the smallest serious APS-C snapshot camera and do not need the Fuji aesthetic package around it.

Final Verdict

The X100VI makes more sense emotionally.

The GR III makes more sense practically.

Neither answer is wrong. The only wrong move is pretending they are basically the same camera, because they really are not.

editor_settings.author: Sophie Tran
editor_settings.meta_description: The Fujifilm X100VI and Ricoh GR III are both cult-favorite compact cameras, but they make sense for very different people. Here is the clearer choice.
